evidence.correctness_and_freshness: client.responses.create with stream=True and event.type checks for response.output_text.delta and response.completed are consistent with the Responses API. However, usage field names used in the React UI (prompt_tokens, completion_tokens, prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens) are Chat Completions naming; Responses API uses input_tokens/output_tokens/input_tokens_details.cached_tokens. Frontend will show undefined.
evidence.executability: All blocks parse and imports are present. Backend should run as-is. Frontend will run but display undefined for token counts due to field name mismatch — a single non-obvious fix.
evidence.completeness: All six pieces present: API key acquisition URL, pip and npm install commands, .env example, backend code, frontend code, runnable test commands (uvicorn + npm run dev). Env var OPENAI_API_KEY consistent.
evidence.citation_anchoring: Only link is platform.openai.com/api-keys (dashboard, not docs). No citation to platform.openai.com/docs for Responses API streaming or events.

rubric_notes: Long-tail handling: internally consistent on event types and SDK calls; minor inconsistency between backend usage.model_dump() (Responses API shape) and frontend field access (Chat Completions shape).
